---
title: "OPALX Documentation"
description: "The unified entry point for OPALX users, physics researchers, and developers."
audience: user
status: current
last-reviewed: "2026-07-22"
page-layout: full
toc: false
number-sections: false
title-block-style: none
---

:::: {.hero}
# Simulate charged-particle accelerators across CPUs and GPUs

OPALX is the performance-portable accelerator simulation framework for
high-statistics, three-dimensional beam dynamics. This manual brings the user
guide, physics models, command reference, and developer handbook into one
searchable source.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Install OPALX](getting-started/index.qmd)
[Start a simulation](getting-started/first-simulation.qmd){.secondary}
:::
::::

## Find your path

:::: {.doc-grid}
::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Start here</span>

### Getting Started

Build OPALX, choose a platform backend, and run a first input file.

[Get started](getting-started/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Run simulations</span>

### User Guide

Learn the input language, beam definitions, tracking workflow, elements, field
solvers, and outputs.

[Read the user guide](user-guide/input-language.qmd)
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Understand the models</span>

### Physics

Follow coordinate conventions, equations of motion, element fields,
collective effects, decay, spin, and photon physics.

[Explore the physics](physics/overview/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Look something up</span>

### Reference

See the commands and elements registered by the current executable, build
configuration, file formats, and generated API.

[Open the reference](reference/commands.qmd)
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Contribute</span>

### Developer Guide

Understand the code layout, build and test workflows, and how to add features
without losing GPU portability.

[Read the developer guide](developer-guide/architecture.qmd)
:::

::: {.doc-card}
<span class="eyebrow">Resolve a problem</span>

### Troubleshooting

Diagnose build and runtime failures, prepare reproducible bug reports, and
check known limitations.

[Troubleshoot OPALX](troubleshooting/common-problems.qmd)
:::
::::

::: {.resource-strip}
**Live resources**
[Generated API](reference/api.qmd)
[Regression-test dashboard]({{< meta regression-tests-url >}})
[OPALX source]({{< meta source-code-url >}})
:::

## Documentation status

::: {.status-strip}
This redesign is an actively curated foundation. Pages marked **current** have
been checked against the present OPALX source. **Experimental** pages describe
implemented work whose interface may still change. **Planned** pages make
documentation gaps visible instead of filling them with unverified OPAL text.
:::

## Acknowledgments and citation

OPALX builds on the original OPAL project and is developed across PSI and its
international accelerator-science partners. Use the
[publications and citation guidance](resources/publications.qmd) when citing
the software. The original OPAL manual remains available as a
[separate historical resource]({{< meta legacy-opal-manual-url >}}).
